title: A harness for every task: dynamic workflows in Claude Code
preview: Last week, we released dynamic workflows in Claude Code. Claude can now write its own harness on the fly, custom-built for the task at hand.

blocks.english: Last week, we released dynamic workflows in Claude Code. Claude can now write its own harness on the fly, custom-built for the task at hand.
blocks.chinese: 上周，我们在 Claude Code 中发布了动态工作流。Claude 现在可以即时编写自己的工具链，为手头的任务量身定制。

blocks.english: While the default Claude Code harness is built for coding, it is also useful for many other types of tasks because, as it turns out, many tasks resemble coding tasks. But there are certain classes of tasks where we have had to build custom harnesses on top of Claude Code to achieve peak performance such as Research, security analysis, agent teams, or Code Review.
blocks.chinese: 虽然 Claude Code 的默认工具链是为编程构建的，但它对许多其他类型的任务也很有用，因为事实证明，很多任务都类似于编程任务。但对于某些特定类别的任务，我们不得不在 Claude Code 之上构建自定义工具链才能达到最佳性能，例如研究、安全分析、智能体团队或代码审查。

blocks.english: Workflows allow you to dynamically create harnesses built on top of Claude Code that enable Claude to solve all of those problems more natively. You can also share and reuse these workflows with others.
blocks.chinese: 工作流允许你动态创建基于 Claude Code 构建的工具链，使 Claude 能够更原生地解决所有这些问题。你还可以与他人分享和复用这些工作流。

blocks.english: In this article, I'll cover my initial workflows experiences and learnings so you can best take full advantage. Keep in mind, best practices are still developing: dynamic workflows often use more tokens and are best suited for complex, high value tasks.
blocks.chinese: 在本文中，我将分享我最初的工作流经验和心得，以便你能充分利用它们。请记住，最佳实践仍在发展中：动态工作流通常会消耗更多 token，最适合用于复杂、高价值的任务。

blocks.english: Example prompts
blocks.chinese: 示例提示

blocks.english: Before diving into the technical details, I'd like to start with several example prompts to get you thinking about the possibilities with workflows:
blocks.chinese: 在深入技术细节之前，我想先给出几个示例提示，让你思考工作流的各种可能性：

blocks.english: "This test fails maybe 1 in 50 runs. Set up a workflow to reproduce it. Form competing theories about the race, and don't stop until one theory survives the evidence."
blocks.chinese: "这个测试大约每 50 次运行会失败 1 次。建立一个工作流来复现它。提出关于竞态条件的多个对立理论，不要停下来，直到有一个理论经受住证据的考验。"

blocks.english: "Using a workflow, go through my last 50 sessions and mine them for corrections I keep making and turn the recurring ones into CLAUDE.md rules"
blocks.chinese: "使用工作流，回顾我最近 50 次会话，挖掘我反复做出的修正，将反复出现的修正转化为 CLAUDE.md 规则。"

blocks.english: "Use a workflow to dig through #incidents in Slack for the past six months and find recurring root causes where nobody has filed a ticket."
blocks.chinese: "使用工作流，挖掘过去六个月 Slack 中 #incidents 频道的内容，找出没有人提交过工单的反复出现的根本原因。"

blocks.english: "Take my business plan and run a workflow where different agents tear it apart from an investor's, a customer's, and a competitor's perspective."
blocks.chinese: "拿出我的商业计划，运行一个工作流，让不同的智能体分别从投资者、客户和竞争对手的角度对其进行剖析。"

blocks.english: "Here's a folder of 80 resumes, use a workflow to rank them for the backend role and double-check the top ten. Interview me using the AskUserQuestion tool for a rubric."
blocks.chinese: "这是一个包含 80 份简历的文件夹，使用工作流为后端岗位对它们进行排名，并对前十名进行复核。使用 AskUserQuestion 工具采访我以获取评分标准。"

blocks.english: "I need a name for this CLI tool. Use a workflow to brainstorm a bunch of options and run a tournament to pick the top 3."
blocks.chinese: "我需要给这个 CLI 工具起个名字。使用工作流进行头脑风暴，想出一堆选项，然后进行淘汰赛选出前 3 名。"

blocks.english: "Use a workflow to rename our User model to Account everywhere."
blocks.chinese: "使用工作流，将我们的 User 模型在所有地方重命名为 Account。"

blocks.english: "Go through my blog post draft and verify every technical claim against the codebase using a workflow, I don't want to ship anything wrong."
blocks.chinese: "使用工作流检查我的博客文章草稿，对照代码库验证每一项技术声明，我不想发布任何错误内容。"

blocks.english: How dynamic workflows work
blocks.chinese: 动态工作流的工作原理

blocks.english: Dynamic workflows execute a javascript file with a few special functions that help spawn and coordinate subagents.
blocks.chinese: 动态工作流通过执行一个 JavaScript 文件来运行，该文件包含几个用于生成和协调子智能体的特殊函数。

blocks.english: Dynamic workflows also include standard JavaScript functions like JSON, Math, and Array, to help process data.
blocks.chinese: 动态工作流还包含标准 JavaScript 函数，如 JSON、Math 和 Array，以帮助处理数据。

blocks.english: It's particularly useful to know that dynamic workflows can decide which models an agent uses and whether subagents are run in their own worktree, allowing Claude to choose the intelligence level and isolation needed.
blocks.chinese: 尤其需要了解的是，动态工作流可以决定智能体使用哪个模型，以及子智能体是否在各自的工作树中运行，从而让 Claude 能够选择所需的智能水平和隔离程度。

blocks.english: If a workflow is interrupted, for example by user action or quitting the terminal, resuming the session will allow the workflow to pick up where it left off.
blocks.chinese: 如果工作流被中断，例如用户操作或退出终端，恢复会话将允许工作流从中断处继续执行。

blocks.english: Why dynamic workflows
blocks.chinese: 为什么需要动态工作流

blocks.english: When you ask the default Claude Code harness to do a task, it needs to both plan and execute in the same context window. For many coding tasks, this is highly effective, but it can break down over long-running, massively parallel, highly structured and/or adversarial tasks.
blocks.chinese: 当你让默认的 Claude Code 工具链执行一个任务时，它需要在同一个上下文窗口中同时进行规划和执行。对于许多编程任务，这非常有效，但对于长时间运行、大规模并行、高度结构化和/或对抗性的任务，这种方式可能会失效。

blocks.english: This is because the longer Claude works on a complex task in a single context window, the more it becomes susceptible to a few specific failure modes:
blocks.chinese: 这是因为 Claude 在单个上下文窗口中处理复杂任务的时间越长，就越容易受到几种特定失败模式的影响：

blocks.english: - **Agentic laziness** refers to when Claude stops before finishing a particularly complex, multi-part task and declares the job done after partial progress, for example addressing 35 of the 50 items in a security review.
blocks.chinese: - **智能体惰性** 是指 Claude 在完成一个特别复杂的多部分任务之前就停下来，在取得部分进展后就宣布任务完成，例如在安全审查中只处理了 50 个项目中的 35 个。

blocks.english: - **Self-preferential bias** refers to Claude's tendency to prefer its own results or findings, especially when asked to verify or judge them against a rubric.
blocks.chinese: - **自我偏好偏差** 是指 Claude 倾向于偏爱自己的结果或发现，特别是在被要求根据评分标准进行验证或评判时。

blocks.english: - **Goal drift** refers to the gradual loss of fidelity to the original objective across many turns, especially after compaction. Each summarization step is lossy, and details like edge-case requirements or "don't do X" constraints can get lost.
blocks.chinese: - **目标漂移** 是指在多轮对话中逐渐偏离原始目标，特别是在压缩之后。每次摘要步骤都是有损的，边缘情况的需求或"不要做 X"等约束可能会丢失。

blocks.english: Creating a workflow helps combat these by orchestrating separate Claude subagents with their own context windows and focused, isolated goals.
blocks.chinese: 创建工作流有助于解决这些问题，通过编排拥有各自上下文窗口和专注、独立目标的多个 Claude 子智能体来实现。

blocks.english: Dynamic vs static workflows
blocks.chinese: 动态工作流与静态工作流

blocks.english: You may have previously created a static workflow using the Claude Agent SDK or claude -p to coordinate multiple instances of Claude Code together.
blocks.chinese: 你可能之前使用过 Claude Agent SDK 或 `claude -p` 创建静态工作流来协调多个 Claude Code 实例。

blocks.english: But because static workflows need to work for all edge cases, they are usually more generic. With Claude Opus 4.8 and dynamic workflows, Claude is now intelligent enough to write a custom harness tailor-made for your use case.
blocks.chinese: 但由于静态工作流需要处理所有边缘情况，它们通常更加通用。借助 Claude Opus 4.8 和动态工作流，Claude 现在已经足够智能，可以为你的用例编写量身定制的工具链。

blocks.english: Helpful patterns when using dynamic workflows
blocks.chinese: 使用动态工作流的实用模式

blocks.english: You can start using dynamic workflows just by asking Claude to make one, or by using the trigger word "ultracode" to ensure that Claude Code creates a workflow.
blocks.chinese: 你可以直接要求 Claude 创建一个动态工作流，或者使用触发词 "ultracode" 来确保 Claude Code 创建工作流。

blocks.english: But building a mental model for how dynamic workflows work will help you understand when to use them and how you might nudge Claude via prompts.
blocks.chinese: 但建立一个关于动态工作流如何工作的思维模型，将帮助你理解何时使用它们，以及如何通过提示来引导 Claude。

blocks.english: There are a few common patterns that Claude might use and compose together when building workflows:
blocks.chinese: 以下是 Claude 在构建工作流时可能使用和组合的几种常见模式：

blocks.english: Classify-and-act
blocks.chinese: 分类然后行动

blocks.english: Use a classifier agent to decide on the type of task, and then route to different agents or behavior based on the task. Or, use a classifier at the end to determine output.
blocks.chinese: 使用分类器智能体来决定任务类型，然后根据任务路由到不同的智能体或行为。或者，在最后使用分类器来决定输出。

blocks.english: Fan-out-and-synthesize
blocks.chinese: 扇出然后综合

blocks.english: Split up a task into many smaller steps, run an agent on each step and then synthesize those results. This is particularly useful for when there are a large number of smaller steps, or when each step benefits from its own clean context window so they don't interfere or cross-contaminate. The synthesize step is a barrier—it waits for all the fan-out agents, then merges their structured outputs into one result.
blocks.chinese: 将一个任务拆分成许多较小的步骤，对每个步骤运行一个智能体，然后综合这些结果。这对于有大量较小步骤的情况特别有用，或者当每个步骤受益于自己干净的上下文窗口，从而不会相互干扰或交叉污染。综合步骤是一个屏障——它等待所有扇出智能体完成，然后将它们的结构化输出合并为一个结果。

blocks.english: Adversarial verification
blocks.chinese: 对抗性验证

blocks.english: For each spawned agent, run a separate spawned agent to adversarially verify its output against a rubric or criteria.
blocks.chinese: 对于每个生成的智能体，运行一个单独的智能体来根据评分标准或准则对其输出进行对抗性验证。

blocks.english: Generate-and-filter
blocks.chinese: 生成然后筛选

blocks.english: Generate a number of ideas on a topic and then filter them by a rubric or by verification, dedupe duplicates and return only the highest quality, tested ideas.
blocks.chinese: 针对一个主题生成多个想法，然后通过评分标准或验证进行筛选，去重并只返回最高质量、经过验证的想法。

blocks.english: Tournament
blocks.chinese: 淘汰赛

blocks.english: Instead of dividing the work, have agents compete on it. Spawn N agents that each attempt the same task using different approaches. Prompts or models then judge the results in a pairwise fashion using a judging agent until you have a winner.
blocks.chinese: 不是分配工作，而是让智能体相互竞争。生成 N 个智能体，各自使用不同方法尝试同一个任务。然后使用评判智能体以成对方式评判结果，直到产生获胜者。

blocks.english: Loop until done
blocks.chinese: 循环直到完成

blocks.english: For tasks with an unknown amount of work, loop spawning agents until a stop condition is met (no new findings, or no more errors in the logs) instead of a fixed number of passes.
blocks.chinese: 对于工作量未知的任务，循环生成智能体直到满足停止条件（没有新的发现，或日志中没有更多的错误），而不是固定次数的遍历。

blocks.english: Use cases
blocks.chinese: 用例

blocks.english: Think creatively of when and how to ask Claude Code to make dynamic workflows. I've found that workflows are sometimes even more useful for non-technical work.
blocks.chinese: 创造性地思考何时以及如何让 Claude Code 创建动态工作流。我发现工作流有时对于非技术性工作甚至更有用。

blocks.english: Migrations and refactors
blocks.chinese: 迁移和重构

blocks.english: Bun was rewritten from Zig to Rust using workflows. You can read more about how that was done in Jarred's X thread.
blocks.chinese: Bun 使用工作流从 Zig 重写为 Rust。你可以在 Jarred 的 X 帖子中了解更多相关信息。

blocks.english: The key is to break down the task into a series of steps that need to be operated on for example callsites, failing tests, modules, etc. Spin off a subagent for every fix in a worktree to make the fix, then have another agent adversarially review, and merge them. Consider telling the agent not to use resource intensive commands so that you can maximally parallelize without running out of resources on your machine.
blocks.chinese: 关键在于将任务分解为一系列需要操作的步骤，例如调用点、失败的测试、模块等。为工作树中的每个修复启动一个子智能体来执行修复，然后让另一个智能体进行对抗性审查，最后合并它们。考虑告诉智能体不要使用资源密集型命令，这样你就可以最大化并行化而不会耗尽机器上的资源。

blocks.english: Deep research
blocks.chinese: 深度研究

blocks.english: We published a deep research skill (/deep-research) inside Claude Code that uses dynamic workflows. Specifically, it fans-out web searches, fetches sources, adversarially verifies their claims, and synthesizes a cited report.
blocks.chinese: 我们在 Claude Code 中发布了一个使用动态工作的深度研究技能（/deep-research）。具体来说，它扇出式进行网络搜索、获取来源、对抗性验证其声明，并综合出一份带引用的报告。

blocks.english: But you may do this sort of research for more than just web searches. For example, asking Claude to compile a status report from context in Slack or to research how a feature works by exploring a codebase in-depth.
blocks.chinese: 但你可能会将这种研究用于不仅仅是网络搜索。例如，让 Claude 从 Slack 的上下文中编写状态报告，或者通过深入探索代码库来研究某个功能的工作原理。

blocks.english: Deep verification
blocks.chinese: 深度验证

blocks.english: On the other hand, if you have a report where you want to check and source every factual claim that it references you may want to generate a workflow which has one agent identify all of the factual claims and then spin off a subagent to check each one in-detail. You could also have a verification agent check the source subagent to make sure its source is high quality.
blocks.chinese: 另一方面，如果你有一份报告，想要检查和引用其中的每一项事实声明，你可能需要生成一个工作流，让一个智能体识别所有事实声明，然后为每一项启动一个子智能体进行详细检查。你还可以让一个验证智能体检查来源子智能体，以确保其来源是高质量的。

blocks.english: Sorting
blocks.chinese: 排序

blocks.english: You may have a list of items that you want to sort by some qualitative measurement that you believe that Claude Code is good at evaluating, for example: support tickets sorted by severity of the bug. But if you try to sort 1000+ rows in one prompt, quality degrades and it won't fit in context. Instead run a tournament, a pipeline of pairwise-comparison agents (comparative judgment is more reliable than absolute scoring), or bucket-rank in parallel then merge. Each comparison is its own agent, so the deterministic loop holds the bracket and only the running order stays in context.
blocks.chinese: 你可能有一个项目列表，想要按照你认为 Claude Code 擅长评估的某种定性标准进行排序，例如：按 bug 严重程度对支持工单进行排序。但如果你尝试在一个提示中对 1000+ 行进行排序，质量会下降且无法放入上下文。相反，可以运行淘汰赛、成对比较智能体的流水线（比较判断比绝对评分更可靠），或并行分桶排序然后合并。每次比较都有自己的智能体，因此确定性循环维护着比赛表，只有执行顺序保留在上下文中。

blocks.english: Memory and rule adherence
blocks.chinese: 记忆和规则遵守

blocks.english: If you have a particular set of rules that you find Claude misses or struggles with, even when put into the CLAUDE.mds, create a workflow with a list of rules that must be checked by verifier agents—one verifier per rule. Creating a skeptic persona subagent to review the rules to make sure they are in line will help avoid too many false positives.
blocks.chinese: 如果你有一组特定规则，发现 Claude 经常遗漏或难以遵守，即使放在 CLAUDE.md 文件中也是如此，可以创建一个工作流，其中包含必须由验证智能体检查的规则列表——每个规则对应一个验证器。创建一个持怀疑态度的人设子智能体来审查规则是否合理，有助于避免过多的误报。

blocks.english: The reverse direction works too: mine your recent sessions and code review comments for corrections you keep making, cluster them with parallel agents, adversarially verify each candidate (would this rule have prevented a real mistake?), and then distill the survivors back into a CLAUDE.md.
blocks.chinese: 反向操作也同样有效：挖掘你最近的会话和代码审查评论中你反复做出的修正，使用并行智能体对它们进行聚类，对抗性验证每个候选（这条规则是否能防止一个真实的错误？），然后将幸存者提炼回 CLAUDE.md 中。

blocks.english: Root-cause investigation
blocks.chinese: 根本原因调查

blocks.english: Debugging works best when you come up with several independent hypotheses and test them, but if you're only using one context window, Claude can run into self-preferential bias.
blocks.chinese: 调试在你提出几个独立假设并进行测试时效果最好，但如果你只使用一个上下文窗口，Claude 可能会遇到自我偏好偏差的问题。

blocks.english: A workflow can structurally prevent this by spinning up agents to generate hypotheses from disjoint evidence. For example, separate agents for logs, files, and data. Each hypothesis can then face a panel of verifiers and refuters.
blocks.chinese: 工作流可以通过启动智能体从不相关的证据中生成假设来从结构上防止这种情况。例如，分别为日志、文件和数据设置独立的智能体。每个假设随后可以面对一组验证者和反驳者。

blocks.english: This isn't just for code. Workflows can be used for sales (why did sales drop in March?), data engineering (why did this pipeline fail?), or any post-mortem exercise.
blocks.chinese: 这不仅仅适用于代码。工作流可以用于销售（为什么三月份销量下降了？）、数据工程（为什么这个管道失败了？）或任何事后分析工作。

blocks.english: Triaging at scale
blocks.chinese: 大规模分类

blocks.english: Every team has a support queue, bug reports, or some other backlog that cannot be fully processed by humans.
blocks.chinese: 每个团队都有一个支持队列、bug 报告或其他无法由人工完全处理的积压工作。

blocks.english: A triage workflow classifies each item, dedupes against what's already tracked, and takes action. This could mean attempting the fix or escalating to a human user.
blocks.chinese: 分类工作流对每个项目进行分类，与已跟踪的内容进行去重，并采取行动。这可能意味着尝试修复或升级给人工用户。

blocks.english: A useful pattern for triage workflows is quarantine. This involves barring the agents that read untrusted public content from taking high-privilege actions, which are instead done by the agents in charge of acting on the information.
blocks.chinese: 分类工作流的一个有用模式是隔离。这涉及禁止读取不受信任的公共内容的智能体执行高权限操作，而由负责对信息采取行动的智能体来执行。

blocks.english: Pair triage workflows with /loop to have Claude do this continuously.
blocks.chinese: 将分类工作流与 /loop 配对使用，让 Claude 持续执行此操作。

blocks.english: Exploration and taste
blocks.chinese: 探索和品味

blocks.english: Workflows can be useful when exploring different approaches to a solution, especially when it is taste based, like design or naming, and would benefit from a rubric.
blocks.chinese: 工作流在探索不同的解决方案时非常有用，特别是当它是基于品味的，如设计或命名，并且会从评分标准中受益。

blocks.english: Try asking Claude to explore a bunch of solutions, and give a review agent a rubric for what a good solution looks like. The task is complete when the review agent feels like it has met the criteria. Solutions can also be ordered or selected via a tournament based on the rubric.
blocks.chinese: 尝试让 Claude 探索一堆解决方案，并给审查智能体一个关于好解决方案是什么样的评分标准。当审查智能体认为标准已满足时，任务即完成。解决方案也可以根据评分标准通过淘汰赛进行排序或选择。

blocks.english: Evals
blocks.chinese: 评估

blocks.english: You can run lightweight evals for particular tasks by spinning off separate agents in a worktree and then spinning off comparison agents to compare and grade the specific outputs against a rubric. For example, evaluating and then refining a skill you've created against a particular criteria.
blocks.chinese: 你可以通过在工作树中启动独立的智能体来对特定任务运行轻量级评估，然后启动比较智能体根据评分标准对特定输出进行比较和评分。例如，评估并完善你创建的技能以满足特定标准。

blocks.english: Model and intelligence routing
blocks.chinese: 模型和智能路由

blocks.english: Create a classifier agent tuned to your tasks that decides which model to use. This can be helpful when your task will involve many tool calls and conducting research prior to execution can identify the best model for the job.
blocks.chinese: 创建一个针对你的任务调优的分类器智能体来决定使用哪个模型。当你的任务涉及许多工具调用时，这会很有帮助，因为执行前的研究可以识别最适合该工作的模型。

blocks.english: For example, the best model for the task "explain how the auth module works" depends on how many files in the auth module there are and the shape of the codebase. A classifier agent can do this research and then route to Sonnet or Opus based on the expected complexity of the task.
blocks.chinese: 例如，"解释 auth 模块如何工作"这个任务的最佳模型取决于 auth 模块中有多少文件以及代码库的结构。分类器智能体可以进行这项研究，然后根据任务的预期复杂度路由到 Sonnet 或 Opus。

blocks.english: When not to use dynamic workflows
blocks.chinese: 何时不使用动态工作流

blocks.english: Workflows are new. While there are many use cases where it will create outsized results, they are not needed for every task and may end up using significantly more tokens.
blocks.chinese: 工作流是新事物。虽然在许多用例中它会产生巨大的成果，但并非每个任务都需要它们，而且可能会消耗明显更多的 token。

blocks.english: It's best to use workflows creatively to push Claude Code in ways that you haven't previously. For regular coding tasks, try and ask yourself: does it really need more compute? For example, most traditional coding tasks do not need a panel of 5 reviewers.
blocks.chinese: 最好创造性地使用工作流，以你之前未曾尝试的方式来推动 Claude Code。对于常规编程任务，试着问自己：它真的需要更多算力吗？例如，大多数传统编程任务不需要一个 5 人的评审团。

blocks.english: Tips for building dynamic workflows
blocks.chinese: 构建动态工作流的技巧

blocks.english: Prompting
blocks.chinese: 提示技巧

blocks.english: Detailed prompting, using the specific techniques we described above, for dynamic workflows creates the best results.
blocks.chinese: 使用我们上面描述的具体技术进行详细的提示，可以为动态工作流创造最佳结果。

blocks.english: Workflows are not just for large tasks. You can prompt the model to use a "quick workflow." For example, you can create a quick adversarial review of an assumption.
blocks.chinese: 工作流不仅适用于大型任务。你可以提示模型使用"快速工作流"。例如，你可以创建一个对假设的快速对抗性审查。

blocks.english: Combine with /goal and /loop
blocks.chinese: 与 /goal 和 /loop 结合使用

blocks.english: When using workflows that can be repeated, for example triage, research, or verification, pair them with /loop to be run at regular intervals, and /goal to set a hard completion requirement.
blocks.chinese: 当使用可以重复的工作流时，例如分类、研究或验证，将它们与 /loop 配对以定期运行，并与 /goal 配对以设置硬性完成要求。

blocks.english: Token usage budgets
blocks.chinese: Token 使用预算

blocks.english: You can set explicit token usage budgets for dynamic workflows to limit how many tokens a task uses. You can prompt it with a budget like: "use 10k tokens," which will set the cap.
blocks.chinese: 你可以为动态工作流设置明确的 token 使用预算来限制任务使用的 token 数量。你可以用类似 "use 10k tokens" 的预算提示它，这将设置上限。

blocks.english: Saving and sharing dynamic workflows
blocks.chinese: 保存和分享动态工作流

blocks.english: You can save workflows by pressing "s" in the workflow menu. You can check these into ~/.claude/workflows or distribute them via a skill.
blocks.chinese: 你可以通过在工作流菜单中按 "s" 来保存工作流。你可以将它们签入 ~/.claude/workflows 或通过技能进行分发。

blocks.english: To share them via a skill, put your JavaScript workflow files in the skill and folder and reference them in the SKILL.MD. To allow for more flexibility, you may want to prompt Claude to think of the workflows in the skill as a template instead of a script that needs to be run verbatim.
blocks.chinese: 要通过技能分享它们，将你的 JavaScript 工作流文件放在技能文件夹中，并在 SKILL.MD 中引用它们。为了允许更大的灵活性，你可能希望提示 Claude 将技能中的工作流视为模板，而不是需要逐字执行的脚本。

blocks.english: A new starting point for discovery
blocks.chinese: 探索的新起点

blocks.english: Workflows are a helpful new way to extend Claude Code. I encourage you to think of them as a starting point to explore new ways to use Claude to help accomplish your tasks. There is still much to discover in how to use them best. Let me know what you find.
blocks.chinese: 工作流是扩展 Claude Code 的一种有用的新方式。我鼓励你将它们视为探索使用 Claude 帮助完成任务新方式的起点。关于如何最好地使用它们，还有很多值得发现的地方。请告诉我你的发现。

blocks.english: *This article was written by Thariq Shihipar and Sid Bidasia, members of technical staff at Anthropic working on Claude Code.*
blocks.chinese: *本文由 Anthropic 负责 Claude Code 的技术人员 Thariq Shihipar 和 Sid Bidasia 撰写。*
